calculations.formula: sum(average_model_allocation * asset_return)
calculations.name: consensus_portfolio_return

calculations.formula: average resolved model portfolio return
calculations.name: average_model_return

calculations.formula: 100 * consensus_return / oracle_return
calculations.name: consensus_capitalbench_score

insights.summary: If the monthly model allocations were averaged into one consensus portfolio, it returned +0.77% versus -1.65% for the S&P 500 and +6.52% for the hindsight best asset.
insights.title: AI consensus portfolio scored 11.8 versus the oracle
insights.why_it_matters: The consensus portfolio tests whether the combined AI view is more useful than any single model's portfolio or the S&P 500 benchmark.

calculations.formula: sum(average_model_allocation * asset_return)
calculations.name: consensus_portfolio_return

calculations.formula: average resolved model portfolio return
calculations.name: average_model_return

calculations.formula: 100 * consensus_return / oracle_return
calculations.name: consensus_capitalbench_score

insights.summary: If the weekly model allocations were averaged into one consensus portfolio, it returned -0.12% versus +2.11% for the S&P 500 and +13.90% for the hindsight best asset.
insights.title: AI consensus portfolio scored -0.8 versus the oracle
insights.why_it_matters: The consensus portfolio tests whether the combined AI view is more useful than any single model's portfolio or the S&P 500 benchmark.

calculations.formula: max(scored asset returns)
calculations.name: oracle_return

calculations.formula: min(scored asset returns)
calculations.name: worst_asset_return

calculations.formula: positive asset count / scored asset count
calculations.name: positive_universe_share

insights.summary: The best scored asset returned +6.52%, the worst returned -13.59%, and +32.50% of the universe was positive. The S&P 500 ranked 27 out of 40 options.
insights.title: Monthly round had +20.12% asset dispersion
insights.why_it_matters: Benchmark difficulty matters because model scores should be interpreted against the opportunity set and the market window they faced.

calculations.formula: max(scored asset returns)
calculations.name: oracle_return

calculations.formula: min(scored asset returns)
calculations.name: worst_asset_return

calculations.formula: positive asset count / scored asset count
calculations.name: positive_universe_share

insights.summary: The best scored asset returned +13.90%, the worst returned -10.31%, and +87.14% of the universe was positive. The S&P 500 ranked 40 out of 70 options.
insights.title: Weekly round had +24.22% asset dispersion
insights.why_it_matters: Benchmark difficulty matters because model scores should be interpreted against the opportunity set and the market window they faced.

calculations.formula: count models with positive allocation to oracle asset
calculations.name: oracle_asset_holder_count

calculations.formula: average allocation to oracle asset across model portfolios
calculations.name: average_oracle_asset_allocation

insights.summary: The hindsight best asset was Healthcare Sector (XLV) at +6.52%. 0 of 4 models held it, with +0.00% average allocation.
insights.title: Models missed the monthly oracle asset
insights.why_it_matters: This shows whether models identified the eventual best asset before scoring, even when portfolio weights were too small to fully capture the oracle return.

calculations.formula: count models with positive allocation to oracle asset
calculations.name: oracle_asset_holder_count

calculations.formula: average allocation to oracle asset across model portfolios
calculations.name: average_oracle_asset_allocation

insights.summary: The hindsight best asset was South Korea Equities (EWY) at +13.90%. 0 of 5 models held it, with +0.00% average allocation.
insights.title: Models missed the weekly oracle asset
insights.why_it_matters: This shows whether models identified the eventual best asset before scoring, even when portfolio weights were too small to fully capture the oracle return.

calculations.formula: average allocation across newest live model portfolios
calculations.name: aggregate_live_allocation

insights.summary: Across the newest live weekly and monthly portfolios, Semiconductors (SMH) is the largest aggregate allocation at +35.00%.
insights.title: Live AI portfolios are concentrated in Semiconductors (SMH)
insights.why_it_matters: This shows the current crowding point in model capital allocation, before the open rounds receive their final market scores.

calculations.formula: 50 + 50 * weighted average asset risk-on loading
calculations.name: live_risk_taking_score

insights.summary: The newest live portfolios have a deterministic risk-taking score of 88.9 out of 100.
insights.title: Live AI risk posture is aggressive
insights.why_it_matters: The score translates allocations into a common risk scale, so readers can see whether models are collectively leaning defensive, balanced, or aggressive.

calculations.formula: average return for confidence >= median confidence
calculations.name: high_confidence_average_return

calculations.formula: average return for confidence < median confidence
calculations.name: low_confidence_average_return

calculations.formula: average CapitalBench Score for confidence >= median confidence
calculations.name: high_confidence_average_capitalbench_score

calculations.formula: average CapitalBench Score for confidence < median confidence
calculations.name: low_confidence_average_capitalbench_score

insights.summary: Across resolved official results, submissions at or above the median confidence of 0.55 averaged -1.62%, while lower-confidence submissions averaged -2.61%.
insights.title: High-confidence model calls have outperformed lower-confidence calls
insights.why_it_matters: Confidence calibration helps readers judge whether model self-reported confidence carries useful information about realized benchmark performance.

calculations.formula: average weekly allocation by asset-risk regime
calculations.name: weekly_top_regime_allocation

calculations.formula: average monthly allocation by asset-risk regime
calculations.name: monthly_top_regime_allocation

insights.summary: The newest weekly portfolios allocate +81.00% to growth and technology, while the newest monthly portfolios allocate +74.00%.
insights.title: Weekly and monthly AI portfolios both favor growth and technology
insights.why_it_matters: Agreement across horizons signals that the current model posture is not just a short-term tactical move.

calculations.formula: allocation weight * asset return
calculations.name: largest_positive_contribution

calculations.formula: allocation weight * asset return
calculations.name: smallest_positive_contribution

insights.summary: In the latest monthly result, Semiconductors contributed +0.77% to Claude Opus 4.7's portfolio. No holding detracted from the portfolio in this one-holding attribution view.
insights.title: Claude Opus 4.7's result was driven by Semiconductors
insights.why_it_matters: Attribution turns a model score into an explanation of which holdings actually helped or hurt the frozen portfolio.

calculations.formula: allocation weight * asset return
calculations.name: largest_positive_contribution

calculations.formula: allocation weight * asset return
calculations.name: smallest_positive_contribution

insights.summary: In the latest weekly result, Aerospace and Defense contributed +0.68% to Claude Opus 4.7's portfolio. No holding detracted; the smallest positive contribution came from Short-Term Treasury Bills at +0.01%.
insights.title: Claude Opus 4.7's result was driven by Aerospace and Defense
insights.why_it_matters: Attribution turns a model score into an explanation of which holdings actually helped or hurt the frozen portfolio.

calculations.formula: average allocation to assets in the top 20% by pre-run 30-day trailing return
calculations.name: allocation_to_top_30d_momentum_quintile

calculations.formula: average allocation to assets in the bottom 20% by pre-run 30-day trailing return
calculations.name: allocation_to_bottom_30d_momentum_quintile

insights.summary: The newest monthly portfolios allocate +67.00% to the top 20% of assets by prior 30-day return. The strongest 30-day asset in the input table was South Korea Equities (EWY).
insights.title: Monthly models are leaning into recent winners
insights.why_it_matters: This measures whether models are chasing recent momentum or allocating away from it before outcomes are known.

calculations.formula: average allocation to assets in the top 20% by pre-run 30-day trailing return
calculations.name: allocation_to_top_30d_momentum_quintile

calculations.formula: average allocation to assets in the bottom 20% by pre-run 30-day trailing return
calculations.name: allocation_to_bottom_30d_momentum_quintile

insights.summary: The newest weekly portfolios allocate +71.00% to the top 20% of assets by prior 30-day return. The strongest 30-day asset in the input table was South Korea Equities (EWY).
insights.title: Weekly models are leaning into recent winners
insights.why_it_matters: This measures whether models are chasing recent momentum or allocating away from it before outcomes are known.

calculations.formula: interim model return - interim S&P 500 return
calculations.name: best_live_alpha

calculations.formula: interim model return - interim S&P 500 return
calculations.name: worst_live_alpha

insights.summary: Using the latest available interim close, GPT-5.5 in CB-2026-05-24-1M is ahead of the S&P 500 by +6.39 percentage points, while GPT-5.5 in CB-2026-06-02-1M is at -6.28 percentage points.
insights.title: GPT-5.5 has the strongest live alpha
insights.why_it_matters: Live alpha is provisional, but it shows how open model portfolios are moving before the final official score.

calculations.formula: cosine similarity between live allocation vectors
calculations.name: closest_pair_cosine_similarity

calculations.formula: 1 - average cosine similarity versus other live model allocation vectors
calculations.name: outlier_average_distance

insights.summary: The closest live allocation pair is Claude Opus 4.7 and Claude Opus 4.8 with +95.05% cosine similarity. The current allocation outlier is GPT-5.5.
insights.title: Live model portfolios are tightly clustered
insights.why_it_matters: Similarity analysis shows whether models are independently converging on the same portfolio or expressing meaningfully different capital-allocation behavior.
